title: Cupido
content:
The solid, solid universe
    Is pervious to Love;
    With bandaged eyes he never errs,
    Around, below, above.
    His blinding light
    He flingeth white
    On God's and Satan's brood,
    And reconciles
    By mystic wiles
    The evil and the good.
meaning:
Ralph Waldo Emerson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Cupido"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
